title: rubus × pustulatiformis
intro: Rubus × pustulatiformis, also known as the black raspberry, is a hybrid plant species that belongs to the Rosaceae family. It is a type of bramble with dark purple, edible fruit that has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Asian medicine. The medicinal properties of Rubus × pustulatiformis include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which are beneficial for various health conditions such as diabetes, cardiovascular diseases, and cancer. The plant can be prepared into herbal remedies like teas, tinctures, and capsules, which are used to treat a range of health issues and provide potential health benefits like improved digestion, reduced inflammation, and enhanced immune function.
